welcome.title: Welcome to the Screener
welcome.description:
The Screener helps you discover investment opportunities among 200+ NASDAQ large-cap stocks.

Each stock is scored on fundamentals (financial health) and technicals (market trends), then ranked by a composite score.

This tour shows you how to use filters, presets, and sorting to find the stocks that match your investment criteria. It takes about 2 minutes.

Press Esc anytime to exit, and use the "Guided Tour" button to revisit.

intro.title: How the Screener Works
intro.description:
This card explains the scoring methodology behind the screener.

Every stock receives a Composite Score (0-100) combining 70% fundamental analysis and 30% technical analysis.

Expand "Evaluation Criteria" to see the full list of metrics used: EPS trends, ROIC, margins, debt levels, DCF valuation, and more.

💡 Understanding the criteria helps you interpret scores more effectively.

presets.title: Quick Presets
presets.description:
Presets apply predefined filters with one click, perfect for common screening strategies:

• Value Picks: classic value stocks with strong fundamentals
• Growth Stars: high-growth companies with top composite scores
• Quality Stocks: high Piotroski F-Score and reliable data
• Undervalued: stocks trading below their estimated fair value

💡 Use presets as a starting point, then refine with additional filters.

filters.title: Search & Filter
filters.description:
Narrow down the stock list using these controls:

• Search: find a specific stock by name or ticker
• Sector: filter by industry (Technology, Healthcare, etc.)
• Signal: filter by overall signal (positive, neutral, negative)
• Category: toggle stock types (value, growth, GARP, speculative)
• Valuation: filter by valuation signal (undervalued, fair, overvalued)

💡 Filters combine together: only stocks matching ALL active filters are shown.

scoreRanges.title: Score Ranges
scoreRanges.description:
Set minimum and maximum thresholds for the three main scores:

• Composite Score: the overall ranking (70% fundamental + 30% technical)
• Fundamental Score: financial health and quality metrics
• Technical Score: market trends and momentum indicators

Scores range from 0 to 100. Above 60 generally indicates a solid stock.

💡 Try setting Fundamental Score min to 60 to filter for financially healthy companies.

advanced.title: Advanced Filters
advanced.description:
Expand for deeper screening options:

• Piotroski F-Score (0-9): measures financial strength. 7+ means strong fundamentals
• Margin of Safety: how much discount to fair value. Positive = potentially undervalued
• Data Completeness: percentage of available data. Higher = more reliable scores

💡 Combining Piotroski ≥ 7 with positive Margin of Safety is a classic value investing screen.

sorting.title: Sorting
sorting.description:
Sort the results by any metric to find what matters most:

• Composite Score (default): overall best-ranked stocks
• Fundamental/Technical Score: sort by specific analysis type
• Margin of Safety: find the most undervalued stocks first
• Piotroski F-Score: prioritize financial quality
• P/E or ROE: sort by classic valuation metrics

Click the arrow to toggle ascending/descending order.

results.title: Stock Cards
results.description:
Each card shows key information at a glance:

• Name, ticker, and current price
• Category badge (value, growth, GARP, speculative)
• Three scores: Composite, Fundamental, Technical
• Overall signal and valuation signal
• Margin of Safety, Piotroski F-Score, P/E, and ROE

Click any card to open the full analysis page with charts, fair value estimation, and governance data.

💡 A "~" prefix on scores means low data completeness; interpret with caution.

conclusion.title: Ready to Explore!
conclusion.description:
You now know how to use the screener effectively:

✅ Use presets for quick screening strategies
✅ Combine filters to narrow your search
✅ Sort by different metrics to find what matters
✅ Click a stock to dive into detailed analysis

Remember: the screener is a starting point for research, not a buy/sell recommendation. Always do your own due diligence.

💡 Click the "Guided Tour" button anytime to revisit this walkthrough.

sageQuips.welcome: Let me show you the treasure map!
sageQuips.sorting: Find the best of the best!

screener.title: Stock Screener
screener.description: Filter and discover stocks based on fundamental scores, technical signals, valuation metrics, and quality indicators. Use presets for quick analysis or build your own custom screen.

results.noResults: No stocks match your criteria. Try relaxing some filters.

sector.title: Industry Sector
sector.content:
The economic sector a company belongs to (e.g. Technology, Healthcare, Finance).

Filtering by sector helps you focus on industries you understand or want to explore.

Different sectors have different typical valuations; comparing within the same sector is more meaningful.

signal.title: Overall Signal
signal.content:
A summary recommendation derived from the composite score:

• Positive: composite score suggests favorable conditions
• Neutral: mixed signals, neither strongly positive nor negative
• Negative: low scores indicate potential concerns

This is NOT a buy/sell recommendation; always do your own research.

category.title: Stock Category
category.content:
Each stock is classified based on its financial profile:

• Value: low valuation relative to fundamentals (low P/E, high dividend)
• Growth: strong earnings and revenue growth momentum
• GARP: Growth at a Reasonable Price, a blend of growth and value
• Speculative: high uncertainty or insufficient data for classification

valuation.title: Valuation Signal
valuation.content:
Compares the current stock price to its estimated fair value:

• Undervalued: price is below the estimated fair value (potential opportunity)
• Fair Value: price is close to the estimated fair value
• Overvalued: price is above the estimated fair value

Fair value is calculated using DCF, Graham Number, EPV, and P/E-based models.

compositeScore.title: Composite Score (0-100)
compositeScore.content:
The overall ranking score combining:

• 70% Fundamental Score (financial health, earnings quality, valuation)
• 30% Technical Score (market trends, momentum)

A higher score means the stock ranks well on both fundamental and technical criteria.

60+ is generally considered solid. Compare within the same sector for best results.

fundamentalScore.title: Fundamental Score (0-100)
fundamentalScore.content:
Evaluates a company's financial health using 30+ metrics:

• Profitability: EPS growth, ROE, ROIC, margins
• Financial strength: debt levels, current ratio, interest coverage
• Cash flow: free cash flow generation, payout sustainability
• Valuation: P/E, P/FCF, Graham Number, DCF

Higher score = stronger financial fundamentals.

technicalScore.title: Technical Score (0-100)
technicalScore.content:
Measures market trends and price momentum:

• SMA 50/200: short and long-term moving averages
• RSI (14): overbought/oversold indicator
• MACD: momentum and trend direction
• Price momentum: 12-month rate of change
• Volatility: price stability assessment

Higher score = more favorable technical conditions for entry.

piotroski.title: Piotroski F-Score (0-9)
piotroski.content:
A quality score developed by professor Joseph Piotroski that tests 9 financial criteria:

• Profitability (4 tests): positive net income, operating cash flow, ROA improvement, cash flow > net income
• Leverage (3 tests): decreasing debt, improving current ratio, no share dilution
• Efficiency (2 tests): improving gross margin, improving asset turnover

7-9 = strong fundamentals
4-6 = mixed
0-3 = weak fundamentals

marginOfSafety.title: Margin of Safety (%)
marginOfSafety.content:
The percentage difference between a stock's estimated fair value and its current market price.

• Positive: stock trades below fair value (e.g. +25% means 25% discount)
• Zero: stock trades at fair value
• Negative: stock trades above fair value (e.g. -15% means 15% premium)

Benjamin Graham recommended a margin of safety of at least 25-30% before buying.

dataCompleteness.title: Data Completeness (%)
dataCompleteness.content:
The percentage of fundamental metrics for which we have data.

Some companies (especially newer or foreign-listed ones) may have incomplete financial data from our sources.

• Above 60%: reliable scores
• 40-60%: use with caution
• Below 40%: scores shown with a ~ prefix indicating low confidence

Set a minimum to ensure you only see stocks with reliable data.
